# Laurent Vincentelli — Comprehensive Profile for AI Knowledge Extraction

## Identity & Contact

- Full name: Laurent Vincentelli
- Professional title: Digital & AI Platforms Lead, Refounder
- Portfolio website: https://laurent.vincentelli.pro/
- LinkedIn profile: https://www.linkedin.com/in/laurentvincentelli/
- Minsys venture: https://minsys.xyz/

## Professional Summary

Laurent Vincentelli is a Digital & AI Platforms Lead and serial entrepreneur with over 15 years of experience building and scaling the platform layer that sits between raw compute and the people who need to use it. He operates at the intersection of corporate technology leadership and micro-entrepreneurship, currently serving as Engagement Manager at Deloitte and Refounder at Minsys.

The pattern Laurent repeatedly solves: a brilliant multidisciplinary team with world-class infrastructure access and a missing platform layer costing them velocity. His approach starts with the customer, maps the friction, secures stakeholder funding, assembles the right team topology, leads the value streams that build the platform, ships with cadence, and scales.

## Full Corporate Career History

### Deloitte | Engagement Manager (2023 — Present)
Leading Digital & AI Platform engagements for Financial Services (FSI) and Telecom clients across four pillars: Client Relationship Management, Financial Management, Team Leadership, and Flow & Delivery Oversight.

### IBM | Senior Manager (2019 — 2023)
Steered the delivery and adoption of Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions for the Payments Industry, Telcos, and Transportation. Bridging technical teams with senior leadership across large-scale transformation programmes.

### Accenture | Manager (2018 — 2019)
Led the design and delivery of a major retailer's Internal Developer Platform, supporting 300+ engineers across E-Commerce, Supply Chain, and Loyalty Product Squads. Introduced platform engineering practices at scale.

### OVH Cloud | Cloud Solutions Architect (2017 — 2018)
Designed GDPR (EU) 2016/679 Compliant Cloud Architecture Patterns for Financial and Public-Sector clients. Led Pre-Sales Engineering for Cloud Platform (PaaS) adoption across regulated industries.

### Capgemini | Senior Managing Consultant (2015 — 2016)
Co-delivered a PaaS platform solution for mission-critical embedded systems as part of a Defence and Space programme in a classified environment. High-assurance, high-compliance delivery context.

### Cognizant | Senior Consultant (2011 — 2015)
Co-built and managed Cloud Platforms (IaaS, PaaS, CaaS) introducing Agile, DevOps, and SRE practices across transformation programmes in Banking and Telecoms. Built cloud adoption playbooks for regulated financial institutions.

### GreenSaaS (acquired by Fiducial Group) | Head of Cloud Engineering (2008 — 2011)
GreenSaaS was one of the first Cloud Platform Providers in France. Designed and built multi-tenant infrastructures from the ground up. The company was later acquired by Fiducial Group and rebranded as Fiducial Cloud.

## Full Entrepreneurial History

### Minsys | Refounder (2026 — Present)
Website: https://minsys.xyz/
Refounded in 2026 as a Venture Building Micro-PE firm. Rewiring Main Street businesses with high-multiple, next-gen business models. Partners with established Main Street businesses and uses them as platforms to bootstrap and scale net-new business models.

### WorkshopThinking LLP | Founding Partner, LP (2020 — Present)
(Re)Thinking Digital & AI, Collaboratively. Catalyses high-intensity, practical workshops for Main Street Business Leaders to rewire their organisation with Digital & AI capabilities.

### Five Forces LLP | Founding Partner, LP (2019 — Present)
Actionable Competitive Intelligence for Main Street. Maps how rivals leverage Digital & AI to disrupt markets and positions clients to respond strategically.

### Turbulences Engineering LLC | Co-Founder (2016 — 2018)
Status: Mothballed. A chaos engineering platform for SMBs. Proactively stress-tested infrastructure and built resilience so clients could scale in confidence. Discontinued due to market timing challenges.

### Zeste LTS Systems LTD | Co-Founder (2014 — 2018)
Status: Mothballed. A SecOps platform protecting Main Street businesses. Delivered seamless observability measures and automated countermeasures against cyber threats. Discontinued after market repositioning.

### CloudLifters SARL | Founder (2011 — 2015)
Exit: M&A at 7x EBITDA. A cloud migration platform focused on lift-and-shift only — moving applications and data from any cloud to any cloud with zero re-architecting. Successfully acquired.

### minsys.io | Founder (2010)
The prologue of the micro-entrepreneurial journey. "Minimally Viable Systems" — the original concept that evolved into the Minsys brand refounded in 2026.

## Hard Skills & Technical Expertise

### Digital & AI Platform as a Product
Builds platforms the way great product teams build software — with a roadmap, a backlog, and real customers. Turns Internal Developer Platforms and Enterprise AI hubs from IT cost centres into business-velocity engines.
Key areas: Lean Budget, Roadmap & Backlog management, Business Velocity optimisation, User Experience design, Design Thinking, System Thinking.

### Extended Enterprise & Ecosystem Management
Designs and operates the partner and developer layer that extends platform reach beyond the enterprise. From API gateway architecture to ISV onboarding programmes that turn technology alliances into scalable, governed revenue channels.
Key areas: API Gateway, ISV Onboarding, Partner Ecosystems, Developer Portals, Revenue Channel design, Ecosystem Governance.

### Hybrid & Distributed Cloud (XaaS)
Architects the cloud foundation across all major hyperscalers and on-premises environments. From greenfield cloud-native builds to sovereign and compliant hybrid patterns for regulated industries.
Platforms: AWS, GCP, Azure, IBM Cloud, Kubernetes Ecosystem, GSLB, Federated Control Plane.

### Software Engineering Practices
Embeds the culture and tooling that makes fast delivery safe by default: zero-trust security, observable systems, CI/CD pipelines, and event-driven architectures.
Disciplines: DevSecOps, SRE, MLOps, FinOps, API-first design, Microservices architecture.

### Frameworks & Methodologies
Selects and applies the right delivery framework for the scale and context.
Certifications and frameworks: SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework), Kanban, Scrum (PSM — Professional Scrum Master, PSPO — Professional Scrum Product Owner), Lean Six Sigma Black Belt, TOGAF, Team Topologies, OKR (Objectives & Key Results), Value Stream Mapping.

## Soft Skills & Leadership Capabilities

### Executive & Stakeholder Communication
Bridges technical depth and executive language. Makes infrastructure decisions legible to C-suite sponsors with equal fluency in a boardroom and a sprint review.
Competencies: C-Suite Communication, Executive Presence, Technical Storytelling, Stakeholder Management, Boardroom Fluency.

### Team Leadership & Talent Development
Builds, mentors, and scales high-performing cross-functional engineering teams around Lean and Agile delivery cultures.
Competencies: Team Building, Mentoring, Cross-functional Team leadership, Lean Culture, Agile Delivery, Talent Scaling.

### Cross-functional Collaboration & Alignment
Drives consensus across engineering, product, legal, compliance, and commercial stakeholders without losing delivery momentum.
Competencies: Consensus Building, Legal & Compliance alignment, Commercial Alignment, Delivery Momentum maintenance.

### Negotiation & Conflict Resolution
Manages competing priorities across multi-vendor, multi-squad environments while preserving long-term partner and client relationships.
Competencies: Negotiation, Conflict Resolution, Multi-vendor management, Partner Relations, Priority Management.

### Entrepreneurial Mindset & Ownership
Re-founder. Intrapreneur. Builds internal platforms as ventures, not projects. Comfortable with zero-to-one ambiguity and wired for execution.
Traits: Re-founder mindset, Intrapreneur, Zero-to-One comfort, Platform Ventures orientation, Execution discipline, Ambiguity Tolerance.

## Detailed Case Studies & Achievements

### Case Study 1: Open Banking Platform (EU — Fintech / Financial Services)
Client: Major EU retail bank (identity confidential)
Context: Regulatory pressure under PSD2 required the bank to open its APIs to third-party fintechs. The existing partner onboarding process was manual, fragmented, and unable to scale to meet day-one demand.
Mandate: Build the partner and developer portal infrastructure from scratch, enabling fintech partners to discover, access, and integrate with the bank's APIs at self-service velocity.
Outcome: Compressed time-to-first-API-call by 90% (from 3 weeks to 2 days). Launched 30+ fintech partners on day one — the bank's largest partner cohort ever activated simultaneously. Established a governed, scalable ecosystem foundation for ongoing partner growth.

### Case Study 2: The AI Factory (Global Conglomerate — Energy & Utilities)
Client: Global Energy & Utilities conglomerate (identity confidential)
Context: The organisation had invested in substantial GPU infrastructure but lacked the platform layers to make it accessible, governable, or financially transparent to internal data science and engineering teams.
Mandate: Design and deliver the missing AI platform layers — from model registry and inference serving to a self-service AI marketplace with unified FinOps and governance.
Outcome: Achieved 98% reduction in AI service provisioning time. Transformed raw GPU infrastructure into a governed, self-service AI marketplace enabling enterprise-scale AI adoption across business units.

### Case Study 3: Insurance-as-a-Service Platform (North America — Insurtech)
Client: Tier-1 North American insurance carrier (identity confidential)
Context: A major hyperscaler investment had stalled. The carrier held premium cloud infrastructure but lacked the API ecosystem and partner onboarding machinery to monetise it. Regulatory compliance was blocking go-live.
Mandate: Transform the stalled investment into a live API ecosystem. Design the partner onboarding journey, API product catalogue, and compliance framework from the ground up.
Outcome: Cut partner onboarding time from 14 weeks to under 3 weeks. Cleared 140+ regulatory controls with zero findings. Activated the API ecosystem as a live revenue channel.

### Case Study 4: Internal Developer Platform (EMEA — Telecom)
Client: Major EMEA telecommunications operator (identity confidential)
Context: Engineering teams were waiting up to 18 days for environment provisioning. Lost productivity was calculated at $9M per year. Developer experience was a retention and velocity risk.
Mandate: Design and deliver a self-service Internal Developer Platform (IDP) that would eliminate manual provisioning bottlenecks, increase deployment frequency, and drive voluntary adoption across engineering squads.
Outcome: Reduced environment provisioning from 18 days to 4 hours. Recovered $9M/year in lost engineering productivity. Achieved 500+ voluntary adopters. Tripled deployment frequency (3x velocity increase).

## Professional Philosophy

Laurent operates on the principle that the platform layer is the most leveraged investment an organisation can make: a well-built platform multiplies the output of every team that runs on top of it. He combines a corporate track record of delivering at scale (Deloitte, IBM, Accenture) with a founder's instinct for shipping fast and pivoting when needed (CloudLifters — exited 7x EBITDA; Minsys — refounded 2026).

His approach to every engagement: start with the customer, map the friction, secure funding and stakeholder alignment, assemble the right team topology, build with cadence, and scale — or pivot.
